title: Love Language Test
description: Answer 30 relationship scenarios to find the affection signal that lands most strongly for you.
instructions: Answer for what makes you feel cared for in a close relationship.
Think about repeated patterns, not one memorable event.
This test describes communication preferences, not relationship compatibility.

scaleQuestions.question: They tell you specifically what they appreciate about you.
They send a thoughtful message before a stressful day.
They say they are proud of how you handled something.
They apologize clearly and name what they will do differently.
They encourage you when you doubt yourself.
They notice your effort and say it out loud.
They put their phone away and give you full attention.
They protect regular time for just the two of you.
They join you in an activity even if it is simple.
They ask follow-up questions and stay present.
They choose shared experiences over multitasking.
They make time for a conversation without rushing it.
They handle a task that has been weighing on you.
They help before you have to ask twice.
They make your day easier in a practical way.
They remember a responsibility and take care of it.
They show support by solving a real-life problem with you.
They follow through on promises without needing reminders.
They bring you a small item that shows they were thinking of you.
They choose a present that reflects your taste or history.
They save something meaningful from a shared moment.
They surprise you with something useful or personal.
They mark important dates with a tangible gesture.
They notice what you like and choose accordingly.
They hug you when words are not enough.
They sit close to you during quiet moments.
They hold your hand in public or private.
They offer comforting touch when you are stressed.
They use physical affection to reconnect after distance.
They make everyday affection feel natural and safe.

WORDS.title: Words of Affirmation
WORDS.summary: You feel loved when care is spoken clearly, specifically, and sincerely.
careers: Use written notes for important moments
relationships: You notice tone, timing, and specificity in language.
growthPlan: Ask: What words help you feel supported?
Ask: Which phrases feel meaningful versus automatic?
Ask: How should we repair careless words?

TIME.title: Quality Time
TIME.summary: You feel loved when someone is present, attentive, and willing to share unhurried time.
weaknesses: Busy seasons may feel like rejection
relationships: You read attention as affection and distraction as distance.
growthPlan: Ask: What counts as quality time for each of us?
Ask: How do we protect time during busy weeks?
Ask: What shared rituals feel realistic?

ACTS.title: Acts of Service
ACTS.summary: You feel loved when care becomes practical help, reliability, and shared responsibility.
weaknesses: Broken promises can feel especially painful
Help may be invisible if not discussed
careers: Ask what help would actually reduce pressure
relationships: You notice whether effort becomes action.
growthPlan: Ask: Which tasks feel most supportive?
Ask: When does help feel caring versus controlling?
Ask: How do we divide invisible labor?

GIFTS.title: Thoughtful Gifts
GIFTS.summary: You feel loved when tangible gestures show memory, attention, and personal meaning.
weaknesses: Expensive gifts are not the point
Others may misread this as materialism
careers: Explain the story behind a gift
relationships: You notice the thought behind the object more than the price.
growthPlan: Ask: What kinds of gestures feel meaningful?
Ask: Which dates or moments matter most?
Ask: How can we keep gifts thoughtful but not pressured?

TOUCH.title: Physical Affection
TOUCH.summary: You feel loved through safe, welcome physical closeness and embodied reassurance.
weaknesses: Physical distance may feel emotionally distant
relationships: You read closeness, warmth, and physical reassurance as care.
growthPlan: Ask: When do you need space instead?
Ask: How should we reconnect after conflict?
